cards.front: What is the primary purpose of a blockchain?
cards.back: To record transactions and data in a secure, decentralized, and transparent manner

A theoretical limit on the scalability of distributed systems, stating that any system can at most achieve two out of the following three properties: consistency, availability, and partition tolerance

cards.front: What is the Byzantine Generals problem?
cards.back: A theoretical problem in distributed systems that describes a scenario where a group of generals are trying to agree on a common action, but some of the generals may be malicious and can send fake messages to the others
A mechanism by which nodes in a distributed system agree on a common state or value, ensuring that all nodes have a consistent view of the system

cards.front: What is the history of blockchain?
cards.back: Blockchain technology was first proposed by an individual or group of individuals under the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008, and was first implemented in 2009 with the launch of Bitcoin
